title: Cacalia neriifolia
taxonomy: Cacalia neriifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the dandelion or sunflower family. It is a perennial herb native to East Asia. The plant's classification is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Asterales, Family: Asteraceae, Genus: Cacalia, Species: C. neriifolia. The plant's scientific name Cacalia neriifolia is used to distinguish it from other species within the same genus. This classification helps in identifying and differentiating the plant from other species.
uses: Cacalia neriifolia is used in traditional medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant is said to have potential in treating various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems. It is also used to treat digestive issues and is believed to have antimicrobial properties. The roots of the plant are used to prepare a medicinal tea, which is consumed to alleviate symptoms of various diseases. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Cacalia neriifolia as a medicinal plant.
